title: HTTP/1.1 Response Serialization, Field Order, Framing, and Writes · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective canonical bounded HTTP/1.1 response serializer; retain the raw transcript, isolate the first divergent state, and preserve a regression corpus entry. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in canonical bounded HTTP/1.1 response serializer and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Commit one status line, validated field section, empty line, framed content, and trailers in protocol order.
Reject CR, LF, NUL, controls, invalid names, and unsafe values before serializing any response field.
Choose no-content, fixed-length, chunked, or close-delimited output from method, status, known length, streaming, version, and reuse policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Response Commit Order
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug protocol team handles case 1223 in a signed webhook receiver behind an HTTP/2 to HTTP/1.1 gateway. Build deterministic Go evidence to select one output framing mode while retaining partial-write and field-validation evidence; preserve this operating constraint: a timeout must reserve cleanup time and retain the terminal phase; then inject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and close before any downstream forwarding. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Commit one status line, validated field section, empty line, framed content, and trailers in protocol order.

Inspect the Response Commit Order trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Response Commit Order.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for commit one status line, validated field section, empty line, framed content, and trailers in protocol order. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Response Commit Order, keep Response Commit Order connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Serializer Roundtrip, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Byte-for-byte round-trip identity is required even for field-name case and permitted whitespace normalization.
